texto: The supernatural Christ of the New Testament, the god of orthodox Christianity, is dead. But priestcraft lives and conjures up the ghost of this dead god to frighten and enslave the masses of mankind. The name of Christ has caused more persecutions, wars, and miseries than any other name has caused. The darkest wrongs are still inspired by it. The wails of anguish that went up from Kishinev, Odessa, and Bialystok still vibrate in our ears.
explicacao: Citação de John E. Remsburg sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “The supernatural Christ of the New Testament, the god of orthodox Christianity, is dead. But prie…” articula motivacao, anguish, bialystok em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: The supernatural Christ of the New Testament, the god of orthodox Christianity, is dead. But priestcraft lives and conjures up the ghost of this dead god to frighten and enslave the masses of mankind. The name of Christ has caused more persecutions, wars, and miseries than any other name has caused. The darkest wrongs are still inspired by it. The wails of anguish that went up from Kishinev, Odessa, and Bialystok still vibrate in our ears.

texto: This doctrine of forgiveness of sin is a premium on crime. 'Forgive us our sins' means "Let us continue in our iniquity." It is one of the most pernicious of doctrines, and one of the most fruitful sources of immorality. It has been the chief cause of making Christian nations the most immoral of nations. In teaching this doctrine Christ committed a sin for which his death did not atone, and which can never be forgiven. There is no forgiveness of sin. Every cause has its effect; every sinner must suffer the consequences of his sins.
explicacao: Orientação direta de John E. Remsburg sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “This doctrine of forgiveness of sin is a premium on crime. 'Forgive us our sins' means "Let us co…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a educacao, biblical-criticism, cause-and-effect, útil em momentos de luto e cause-and-effect.
